message:
test(fuzz): cover all_shortest_paths bellman-ford in fuzz_shortest_path

Adds two new ShortestPathInput variants
(AllShortestPathsBellmanFordUndirected, AllShortestPathsBellmanFordDirected)
exercising the new
all_shortest_paths_weighted_bellman_ford{,_directed} entry points
landed in 887e5230. For each generated weighted graph the fuzzer:

- treats Err(()) as a permitted negative-cycle outcome and skips
- on Ok(paths), validates every returned path is a valid edge sequence
  source→…→target in the graph
- when all weights are non-negative, asserts every path's total weight
  matches the brute-force Bellman-Ford-relaxation distance from source
  to target (i.e. all returned paths really are shortest)

Negative-edge undirected inputs are accepted structurally but skipped
for the brute-force comparison since the brute relaxes both directions
and would diverge on negative weights (the implementation under test
correctly raises Err in those cases on the undirected side, treating
any negative undirected edge as an implicit negative cycle).

cargo check on the fnx-fuzz workspace passes cleanly.
